title: Apocopis courtallumensis
taxonomy: Apocopis courtallumensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass. The species was first described by V.J.Nair and S.K.Bhansali in 1989. The classification of Apocopis courtallumensis is not well-documented in widely available sources, but it appears to be part of the Poaceae family and possibly related to other species within the Apocopis genus. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact classification.

intro: Apocopis courtallumensis, commonly known as Courtallum grass, is a plant species belonging to the Poaceae family, native to the Western Ghats region of India. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Apocopis courtallumensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into various Ayurvedic and Unani medicinal formulations.
